truth.papers.A16.title: Bi-directional ferrite to austenite transformation through warm temperature deformation of a ferrite-based low density steel
truth.papers.A16.paper_scope: Investigating bi-directional deformation-induced ferrite-to-austenite and austenite-to-ferrite transformations in Fe-11.15Mn-5.6Al-0.07C low density steel through warm compression tests and thermodynamic Gibbs energy modeling.

prediction.papers.A16.title: Bi-directional ferrite to austenite transformation through warm temperature deformation of a ferrite-based low density steel
prediction.papers.A16.paper_scope: Investigation of bi-directional ferrite-austenite deformation-induced transformation and its thermodynamic interpretation in Fe-11.15Mn-5.6Al-0.07C low density steel during warm compression.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe11.15mn5.6al0.07c.alloys_: Electroslag remelted (ESR) condition; homogenized at 1200°C for 1h, hot-rolled 5 passes 1100–1200°C, air quenched, annealed at 950°C for 1h to obtain triplex microstructure.

truth.processes.proc_annealed_triplex.descript: Homogenization (1200°C/1h) + hot rolling (5 passes, 1100–1200°C, air quench) + annealing (950°C/1h) resulting in triplex ferrite+austenite+martensite microstructure.
truth.processes.proc_annealed_triplex.processe: Initial material state prior to compression.
truth.processes.proc_500C_0.3.description: Warm compression at 500°C to true strain 0.3 at strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹, preceded by soaking at 500°C for 5 min, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_500C_0.6.description: Warm compression at 500°C to true strain 0.6 at strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹, preceded by soaking at 500°C for 5 min, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_600C_0.6.description: Warm compression at 600°C to true strain 0.6 at strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹, preceded by soaking at 600°C for 5 min, water quenched.

prediction.processes.proc_annealed_triplex.descript: Homogenization at 1200 °C for 1 h, hot-rolled through 5 passes at 1100–1200 °C, air quenching, and annealing at 950 °C for 1 h to obtain a triplex microstructure.
prediction.processes.proc_500C_0.6.description: Compression at 500 °C under a strain rate of 0.001 s−1 to a true strain of 0.6 followed by immediate water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_600C_0.6.description: Compression at 600 °C under a strain rate of 0.001 s−1 to a true strain of 0.6 followed by immediate water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_500C_0.3.description: Compression at 500 °C under a strain rate of 0.001 s−1 to an interrupted true strain of 0.3 followed by immediate water quenching.

truth.processing_steps.sample_500C_0.3.process: strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹, water quenched immediately after deformation
truth.processing_steps.sample_500C_0.6.process: strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹, water quenched immediately after deformation
truth.processing_steps.sample_600C_0.6.process: strain rate 0.001 s⁻¹, water quenched immediately after deformation

To attain a triplex (ferrite + austenite + martensite) microstructure.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_500C_0.6.process: Strain rate 0.001 s−1 to true strain 0.6; immediate water quenched to preserve deformed microstructure.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_600C_0.6.process: Strain rate 0.001 s−1 to true strain 0.6; immediate water quenched to preserve deformed microstructure.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_500C_0.3.process: Strain rate 0.001 s−1 to interrupted true strain 0.3; immediate water quenched to preserve deformed microstructure.

truth.structures.sample_initial_triplex.overal: Triplex microstructure composed of ferrite, austenite, and thermally induced martensite with extremely low dislocation density.
truth.structures.sample_500C_0.3.overall_struc: Partially deformed microstructure with dislocation cells and HDDWs in ferrite, planar slip character, and fresh ferrite grains formed in austenite by strain-induced austenite-to-ferrite transformation.
truth.structures.sample_500C_0.3.microstructur: deformed, containing dislocation cells and HDDWs
fresh grains formed within prior austenite via DIFT
truth.structures.sample_500C_0.6.overall_struc: Banded structure in ferrite, sheared parent austenite, and simultaneous deformation-induced ferrite-to-austenite (DIAT) and austenite-to-ferrite (DIFT) transformations along deformation bands.
truth.structures.sample_500C_0.6.microstructur: freshly formed via austenite-to-ferrite transformation along bands
freshly formed via ferrite-to-austenite transformation along bands
truth.structures.sample_600C_0.6.overall_struc: Uniformly deformed microstructure without pronounced banding or deformation-induced transformations; higher temperature enabled better strain distribution.
truth.structures.sample_600C_0.6.microstructur: uniformly deformed, no notable fresh ferrite

prediction.structures.sample_initial_triplex.overal: Triplex microstructure consisting of ferrite, austenite and thermally induced martensite.
prediction.structures.sample_500C_0.6.overall_struc: Triplex microstructure with frequently distributed deformation bands and simultaneous ferrite-to-austenite and austenite-to-ferrite transformations along the bands.
prediction.structures.sample_600C_0.6.overall_struc: Triplex microstructure without pronounced deformation bands due to higher capability for strain distribution at 600 °C.
prediction.structures.sample_500C_0.3.overall_struc: Ferrite with dislocation cells, slip bands and HDDWs; fresh ferrite grains formed within prior austenite.

truth.interfaces.sample_500C_0.6.defect_intera: shear strain localization along deformation bands
Shear strain localization along deformation bands released by simultaneous bidirection transformation (DIFT and DIAT), reducing flow localization and causing flow softening.
truth.interfaces.sample_500C_0.6.interface_not: Local stress/strain at band interfaces triggers transformation.
Deformation bands propagate and shear the parent austenite, with transformation accommodating slip.
truth.interfaces.sample_500C_0.6.phase_evoluti: Simultaneous ferrite→austenite and austenite→ferrite transformations due to equal Gibbs free energy of both phases resulting from Mn/Al partitioning.

prediction.interfaces.sample_500C_0.6.interface_not: Shear strain localization along deformation bands is released through simultaneous ferrite-to-austenite and austenite-to-ferrite transformations.
prediction.interfaces.sample_500C_0.6.phase_evoluti: Propagation of deformation bands shears parent austenite and induces bidirectional transformation as a strain compensation mechanism.
prediction.interfaces.sample_500C_0.3.phase_evoluti: Strain-induced austenite to ferrite transformation occurs within prior austenite islands at interrupted strain of 0.3.

truth.properties.sample_500C_0.3.mechanical.te: True stress-strain curve at 500°C up to strain 0.3 (interrupted), presented in Fig.1c.
truth.properties.sample_500C_0.6.mechanical.te: True stress-strain curve at 500°C to strain 0.6, shown in Fig.1c; observed flow softening after strain 0.3.
truth.properties.sample_600C_0.6.mechanical.te: True stress-strain curve at 600°C to strain 0.6, shown in Fig.1c; higher stress level than at 500°C for the same strain range (no flow softening).

truth.characterization_methods.sample_initial_: detailed observation of phases and dislocation density
Extremely low dislocation density in ferrite and austenite; clear identification of martensite via SAED.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_500C_0.6: reveal banded structure and bidirectional transformation at strain 0.6
Banded structure within ferrite, shearing of parent austenite, simultaneous ferrite→austenite (DIAT) and austenite→ferrite (DIFT) transformations along deformation bands.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_600C_0.6: check for deformation bands or transformation at 600°C
No characteristics of deformation-induced transformations observed at 600°C.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_500C_0.3: analyze dislocation configurations and strain-induced transformation at interrupted strain 0.3
Dislocation cells and high dislocation density walls (HDDWs) within ferrite, multiple slip traces, and fresh ferrite grains within austenite (austenite→ferrite transformation).

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_initial_: Phase identification via selected area diffraction patterns
Verified existence of thermally induced martensite formed during previous annealing.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_500C_0.6: Chemical composition analysis of constituent phases after deformation
Elemental partitioning of Mn and Al between ferrite and austenite after deformation at 500 °C consistent with Thermo-Calc predictions.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_600C_0.6: Phase mapping and microstructural characterization after deformation at 600 °C
No pronounced deformation bands observed; triplex microstructure retained after deformation to true strain 0.6.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_500C_0.3: Dislocation configuration and phase transformation analysis at interrupted strain 0.3
Dislocation cells and multiple slip traces within ferrite; fresh ferrite grains formed within prior austenite indicating strain-induced austenite-to-ferrite transformation.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: CALPHAD-based thermodynamic calculation of phase equilibria and Gibbs free energy
truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Elemental partitioning from EDS (Table 1: ferrite Mn 10.3±0.1 wt%, Al 6.0±0.1 wt%; austenite Mn 12.8±0.1 wt%, Al 4.7±0.1 wt%) matched Thermo-Calc predictions; local Gibbs energy equality at partitioned composition confirmed thermodynamic driving force for bidirectional transformation.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: interpret kinetic of phase transformation and phase stability in multicomponent systems

Gibbs free energy of BCC (ferrite) and FCC (austenite) computed as function of Mn and Al content at 500°C and 550°C; point of equal energy indicates thermodynamic condition for coexistence and bidirectional transformation.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Predicted Mn and Al partitioning compared with SEM-EDS measured compositions in Table 1 and Fig. 4a-b.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Interpretation of the kinetics of phase transformation in multicomponent alloying systems
Calculated Gibbs free energy of BCC and FCC phases as a function of Mn and Al content at 500 °C and 550 °C; point A indicates equal Gibbs free energy for both phases under partitioned compositions.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The initial triplex microstructure exhibited extremely low dislocation density in both ferrite and austenite.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Flow softening after true strain 0.3 at 500–600°C was attributed to deformation-induced transformations, not to dynamic recrystallization or extended dynamic recovery.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Deformation bands at 500°C led to partitioning of ferrite and shearing of parent austenite; shear strain localization was released by simultaneous ferrite→austenite and austenite→ferrite transformations.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: At 600°C, the absence of banded structure and transformations was attributed to higher strain distribution capability at elevated temperature.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Mn partitioning (higher in austenite) primarily dictated phase stability and Gibbs energy equality, while Al partitioning (higher in ferrite) had negligible influence on phase stability.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The compressive stress-strain curves exhibited mechanical stability in the 200–500°C range, with a significant stress drop upon increasing temperature from 500 to 600°C.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The Gibbs free energy model (Thermo-Calc) showed that local elemental partitioning (Mn, Al) brought ferrite and austenite to the same Gibbs energy level, enabling bidirectional transformation.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The as-received material was in an electroslag remelted (ESR) condition before homogenization.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: True-stress-strain curves under compression exhibit mechanical stability over 200–500 °C and a significant stress drop from 500 to 600 °C; appreciable flow softening after true strain of 0.3 at 500–600 °C is attributed to strain-induced transformations. Quantitative flow stress values were not provided in the text (tabular values in the supplied Fig. 1d appear to be pipeline artifacts).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Chemical composition of constituent phases after deformation at 500 °C measured by SEM-EDS: ferrite contains 10.3 ± 0.1 wt.% Mn and 6.0 ± 0.1 wt.% Al; austenite contains 12.8 ± 0.1 wt.% Mn and 4.7 ± 0.1 wt.% Al.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The stacking fault energy of the steel is reported as 80–110 mJ/m2, which theoretically suppresses austenite-to-martensite transformation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Active slip systems in ferrite during warm deformation at 500 °C were identified as {110}<111> and {112}<111>, promoting slip-band intersection and formation of high-dislocation-density walls (HDDWs).
